reasoning: The prompt does not list any specific resolution dates, so I am forecasting for the close date of 2026-01-24. The average temperature for Bourges in late October is approximately 11°C, while in late January it is around 4.5°C. The daily standard deviation of average temperature in these seasons is roughly 4 to 5°C. Assuming two roughly normal distributions of N(11, 4.5^2) and N(4.5, 4.5^2) for late October and late January respectively, the difference (Jan minus Oct) will have a mean of -6.5°C and a standard deviation of sqrt(4.5^2 + 4.5^2) ≈ 6.36°C. The probability of the difference being greater than 0 is roughly the area under the standard normal curve beyond Z = -1.02, which is roughly 15-16%.
